description: Replace SQLite3 stub with a real functional embedded SQL database engine implementing the sqlite3 C API. The implementation must support CREATE TABLE, INSERT, SELECT (WHERE, ORDER BY, LIMIT), UPDATE, DELETE, DROP TABLE with B-tree storage, transactions, prepared statements, parameter binding, thread-safety, and file persistence.
steps: Replace third_party/sqlite/src/sqlite3_stub.cpp with a real implementation (~2000-3000 lines) that implements the sqlite3 C API defined in third_party/sqlite/include/sqlite3.h
Implement an in-memory B-tree index structure for table storage with nodes containing keys and row pointers
Implement a SQL tokenizer/lexer that breaks SQL strings into tokens (keywords, identifiers, operators, literals, punctuation)

Implement a SQL parser that handles: CREATE TABLE (column name, type, PRIMARY KEY, NOT NULL constraints), INSERT INTO...VALUES, SELECT...FROM...WHERE...ORDER BY...LIMIT, UPDATE...SET...WHERE, DELETE FROM...WHERE, DROP TABLE, BEGIN/COMMIT/ROLLBACK, PRAGMA statements

Implement a query executor that: creates tables with schema metadata, inserts rows validating constraints, selects rows with WHERE filtering (=, !=, <, >, <=, >=, IS NULL, IS NOT NULL, LIKE, AND, OR), ORDER BY (ASC/DESC), LIMIT, evaluates expressions
Implement the sqlite3 connection object (struct sqlite3) containing: database tables map, error message, last insert rowid, changes count, open state, mutex for thread-safety
Implement sqlite3_open/sqlite3_open_v2 to create database state, optionally loading from a file
Implement sqlite3_close/sqlite3_close_v2 to persist to file and clean up
Implement sqlite3_exec to parse SQL, execute, and call the callback with results
Implement sqlite3_prepare_v2 to parse SQL into a prepared statement (struct sqlite3_stmt) holding parsed AST, bound parameters array, result cursor
Implement sqlite3_step to execute the prepared statement one row at a time (returning SQLITE_ROW for each result row, SQLITE_DONE when complete)
Implement sqlite3_bind_* functions to set parameter values on prepared statements
Implement sqlite3_column_* functions to read result values from the current row
Implement transactions: BEGIN creates a snapshot, COMMIT applies changes, ROLLBACK reverts to snapshot
Implement file persistence: binary format with header magic bytes, table schemas, and row data. Load on open, save on close or COMMIT
Implement PRAGMA handling: journal_mode=WAL (accept but no-op for compatibility), foreign_keys=ON (store flag)
Ensure thread-safety with a mutex protecting all database operations

Ensure existing tests pass - the ConnectionManager.cpp uses sqlite3_open_v2, sqlite3_prepare_v2, sqlite3_step, sqlite3_column_*, sqlite3_bind_*, sqlite3_finalize, sqlite3_exec, sqlite3_errmsg, sqlite3_last_insert_rowid, sqlite3_changes, sqlite3_close_v2
acceptance_criteria: cmake --build build compiles cleanly with zero errors
ConnectionManager can open a database, create tables, insert rows, and query them back
Transactions work: BEGIN/COMMIT persists data, BEGIN/ROLLBACK reverts
Prepared statements with parameter binding work correctly
Multiple data types supported: INTEGER, TEXT, REAL, BLOB, NULL
PRIMARY KEY and NOT NULL constraints are enforced
Thread-safe access from multiple threads does not cause data corruption
verification: cd /projects/sandbox/ThreatOne && cmake -B build -DENABLE_QT=OFF && cmake --build build 2>&1 | tail -5
